verse_text: This being so, I myself always strive to have a conscience without offense toward God and men.

links.context: Virtue, more than anything else, attracts the gift of immortality. The Church sees in this verse a commentary on the splendor of virginity and the worthiness of the monastic calling. The seven great virtues are humility, liberality, chastity, mildness, temperance, happiness, and diligence. See also [[ACT.24:16]].

symbolic.title: Returning to the Garden of Eden
